LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT CRAIGSLIST, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 2 BOTH BECAUSE THE CLAIM IS BARRED BY SECTION 230 AND BECAUSE PLAINTIFF LACKS STANDING. OVERRULED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1 BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLEADED AN AGREEMENT SUPPORTED BY PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL. 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. FURTHER, DEFENDANT CRAIGSLIST, INC'S ORAL REQUEST FOR A STAY OF DISCOVERY IS GRANTED FOR 2 WEEKS TO PERMIT DEFENDANT TO SEEK A WRIT AND GIVE TIME TO THE COURT OF APPEAL SHOULD IT CHOOSE TO ACCEPT THAT WRIT AND EXTEND A STAY, SHOULD IT CHOOSE TO DO SO. ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT CRAIGLIST, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS FOSTER DAIRY FARMS AND ALBERT CARRENO'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE FROM SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY TO KERN COUNTY IS GRANTED. THE CASE IS TRANSFERRED TO KERN COUNTY. THIS MOTION HAS BEEN BROUGHT PROMPTLY AFTER THE FUNDAMENTAL CHANGE IN CONDITIONS, SPECIFICALLY THE DISMISSAL OF THE ONE DEFENDANT ON WHICH VENUE IN SAN FRANCISCO WAS PREMISED BY VIRTUE OF AN UNCONTESTED MSJ WHICH IS INDICATIVE OF THAT DEFENDANT NOT HAVING BEEN PROPERLY SUBJECT TO LIABILITY. AS THE CASE NOW STANDS, THE CONVENIENCE OF THE WITNESSES AND THE INTERESTS OF JUSTICE FAVOR A TRANSFER TO THE COUNTY WHERE THE CONDUCT AND THE INJURY OCCURED AND WHERE THE OVERWHELMING NUMBER OF THIRD-PARTY WITNESSES ARE LOCATED AND AWAY FROM THE COUNTY WITH NO CONTINUING CONNECTION TO THE CASE, NOTWITSTANDING THE PROXIMITY OF TRIAL. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER, CAROL KAREN CSR #8189

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-06-2011 Text Ruling

DEFENDANST FOSTER DAIRY FARMS AND ALBERT CARRENO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR IN LIGHT OF THE TRANSFER. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter, Carol Karen CSR # 8189 301/PJB

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue From San Francisco County To Kern County; Request For Judicial Notice;
Off Calendar Jun-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:

DEFENDANTS FOSTER DAIRY FARMS AND ALBERT CARRENO'S Motion To Change Venue From San Francisco County To Kern County IS GRANTED. THE CASE IS TRANSFERRED TO KERN COUNTY. THIS MOTION HAS BEEN BROUGHT PROMPTLY AFTER THE FUNDAMENTAL CHANGE IN CONDITIONS, SPECIFICALLY THE DISMISSAL OF THE ONE DEFENDANT ON WHICH VENUE IN SAN FRANCISCO WAS PREMISED BY VIRTUE OF AN UNCONTESTED M S J WHICH IS INDICATIVE OF THAT DEFENDANT NOT HAVING BEEN PROPERLY SUBJECT TO LIABILITY. AS THE CASE NOW STANDS, THE CONVENIENCE OF THE WITNESSES AND THE INTERESTS OF JUSTICE FAVOR A TRANSFER TO THE COUNTY WHERE THE CONDUCT AND THE INJURY OCCURED AND WHERE THE OVERWHELMING NUMBER OF THIRD-PARTY WITNESSES ARE LOCATED AND AWAY FROM THE COUNTY WITH NO CONTINUING CONNECTION TO THE CASE, NOTWITSTANDING THE PROXIMITY OF TRIAL. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter, Carol Karen CSR # 8189 301/PJB

This matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. No opposition filed. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-02-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CRAIGSLIST, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Dawn M. Smith of the Cifarelli Law Firm (with Thomas Cifarelli appearing via Court Call) (714.547.3992) for plaintiff and responding party
Elizabeth L. McDougall and Philip A. Leider of Perkins Coie LLP (344.7168) for defendant Craigslist, Inc.

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: sustained without leave to amend as to cause of action 2 both because the claim is barred by section 230 and because plaintiff lacks standing. Overruled as to cause of action 1 because plaintiff has sufficiently pleaded an agreement supported by promissory estoppel. 10 days to answer. Further, defendant Craigslist, Inc's oral request for a stay of discovery is granted for 2 weeks to permit defendant to seek a writ and give time to the Court of Appeal should it choose to accept that writ and extend a stay, should it choose to do so. Ms. McDougall to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189
